title: Requirements Design and Code Public Checklist

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §5.1 and §11.9, do your high-level software requirements:
records.query: Say what the software must do, developed from the system requirements allocated to software?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §5.1 and §11.9, do your high-level software requirements:
records.query: Read as verifiable, consistent, and conflict-free — so each one can actually be tested?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §5.1 and §11.9, do your high-level software requirements:
records.query: Capture derived requirements (ones that do not trace up to a system requirement) and feed them back to the system safety assessment?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §5.2 and §11.10, does your design:
records.query: Describe the software architecture and the low-level requirements from which code can be written?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §5.2 and §11.10, does your design:
records.query: Address how components interact — data flow, control flow, and scheduling?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §5.2 and §11.10, does your design:
records.query: Handle derived low-level requirements and feed them to the system safety assessment?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §5.3 and §11.11, is your source code:
records.query: Developed directly from the low-level requirements and the software architecture?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §5.3 and §11.11, is your source code:

records.query: Can you trace from system requirements to software requirements to design to code — and back the other way?
Does every piece of code exist because a requirement asked for it, with no unintended or untraceable code?
notes: This free checklist is a high-level sanity check — free to use and share. It doesn't include the item-level actions needed to fully close each objective; those live in AeroCert's comprehensive checklist, kept current as the standards change and available from AeroCert. We'd love to hear how it worked for you — tell us at support@aerocert.org.
